agent_raw: This annual report recommends operationalizing the journal entry anomaly model, but the evaluation design does not support this conclusion. The fundamental problem is that the comparison uses Q1-Q2 as the legacy baseline period and Q3-Q4 as the model-assisted period. The report itself notes that the accounting environment is highly cyclical — entry volume increases 2.3x during close periods, and year-end close produces a structurally different mix of complex entries. The Q4 period, identified as the model's strongest quarter, is the annual close — when escalation-worthy entries are most concentrated regardless of model performance. Simultaneously, a dual sign-off requirement for manual entries above $50,000 was introduced after Q2, changing the posting population in H2 relative to H1. These two factors — seasonality and control change — both push measured precision upward in H2 independent of any model contribution. Additionally, audit reviewed 100% of model-flagged entries but only sampled non-flagged entries, making recall unmeasurable. Before the operationalization recommendation can be supported, a within-period parallel evaluation comparing model and legacy screen on the same entry population, under stable posting controls, with independent adjudication is required.
all_issues_raised: Temporal confound: Q1-Q2 legacy baseline vs Q3-Q4 model period with 2.3x volume surge and year-end close mix
Q2 dual sign-off control change alters H2 entry population concurrent with model deployment
Asymmetric review coverage: 100% flagged vs. sample-only non-flagged — recall unmeasurable
No same-period holdout: model and legacy never evaluated on identical entry population

empirical_test.condition: Within-period parallel comparison of model vs. legacy screen on identical Q3 or Q4 entry populations under stable posting controls and independent blinded adjudication
empirical_test.supports_critique_if: In a same-close parallel evaluation with stable posting controls and independent adjudication, the reported precision edge disappears or is largely reproduced by process changes alone
empirical_test.supports_defense_if: During one common close cycle with unchanged review rules and independent adjudication, the model materially outperforms the legacy screen on escalation yield and precision
empirical_test.ambiguous_if: The model improves queue efficiency somewhat, but most of the annual precision change is explained by year-end seasonality, altered controls, and review-process differences
